frameworks.name: categorical bias in visual working memory
frameworks.one_line: WM representations are repulsed by category boundaries of the stimulus space

frameworks.name: inter-item bias
frameworks.one_line: concurrently held WM items repel each other in feature space

frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: current report is biased by the previous-trial stimulus or response

core_question: How do categorical bias, inter-item bias, and serial dependence interact within visual working memory?
key_assumptions: orientation delayed estimation indexes WM contents
the three biases can be measured by congruent vs incongruent trial sorting
biases combine on a common feature dimension
manipulations: set size two vs set size one orientation delayed estimation
congruent vs incongruent direction of paired biases on a target item
key_findings: categorical bias was completely canceled out by inter-item bias on incongruent trials in Experiment 1
serial dependence was exaggerated on congruent trials in Experiment 2
serial dependence was reduced or canceled on incongruent trials in Experiment 2
interpretations: the three WM biases interact rather than summing independently
the net bias on a remembered item depends on directional alignment of co-acting bias sources
